article_meta.title: Classes of more than 70 students amid historic declines in test scores 02:12 â¢ Source: CNN Classes o

reasoning: The summary maintains neutral, attributed language and avoids the source’s loaded or emotive phrasing. It accurately cites the NWEA study, refrains from adopting opinionated metaphors or adjectives, and proportionally represents the limited perspectives included in the article.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs (e.g., ‘reveals,’ ‘highlights,’ ‘urges’) and avoids charged terms from the source.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All findings are attributed to the NWEA study; no independent assertions are made.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments are presented as facts; evaluative statements are tied to NWEA or its spokesperson.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarming language; avoids the source’s hyperbolic headline framing.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks or character judgments.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No inferences about intentions or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Reflects the single primary perspective (NWEA) without overemphasis or added advocacy.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes all key perspectives present (NWEA, quoted researcher, outlet).
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Adjectives are descriptive and factual (e.g., ‘additional,’ ‘persistent’) rather than value-laden.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Tone matches the study’s findings without amplifying negativity or omitting mitigating context.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No preferential treatment of elite vs. grassroots sources; reflects what the article includes.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Avoids adopting ‘historic declines’ and advocacy metaphors; uses clinical phrasing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures NWEA and the quoted co-author; no missing viewpoints given the article’s content.
